zh.Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England: 英國坎布利其爾藍普頓
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot於1110年之前與Richard Picot在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England: 英国坎布利其爾藍普頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot于1110年之前与Richard Picot在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Picot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Jacques, Jeanpaul, Luc, Pascal, Yves.  Spanish Esperanza, Aida, Alicia, Ana, Felipe, Luis.
French: from Old French picot ‘pointed object, pickaxe’, a nickname for someone who used such an implement. This surname is also found in Haiti.
Catalan: nickname from Valencian dialect picot ‘(sheep or goat) bell’.

variants.name: Pickett
meanings: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Picot, Pigot, a pet form of Pic (see Pike 6). In Middle English, the form Piket (Old French Picquet) was also common.
sometimes a late development of   Pickard   1 or 2.

variants.name: Picotte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Honore, Andre, Jacques, Serge.
Altered form of French Picot , reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot married Richard Picot before 1110, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot a épousé Richard Picot avant 1110, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot hat vor 1110 in England Richard Picot geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot lei sposato Richard Picot prima del 1110, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England: イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアランプトン
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picotは1110年以前にイギリスでRichard Picotと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot(은)는 1110년 이전 영국에서 Richard Picot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot casou-se com Richard Picot antes 1110, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Picot перед 1110 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Picot se casó con Richard Picot antes de 1110, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
